scenario_results.reason: Normal in-scope handling advanced only when PMS, CRM, case-management, discharge transport, payer authorization, and service-recovery evidence aligned, while preserving clinical boundaries.
Missing identity, consent, reservation, authorization, and service-date facts produced a blocked-state request-resolution summary instead of unsafe PHI disclosure or guesswork.
Fee waiver, refund, upgrade, and expired payer-authorization requests were treated as approval-bound exceptions rather than direct lane authority.
Coverage, referral, authorization, and service-setting prerequisites blocked confirmation and routed the case to patient access or payer operations.
PHI disclosure and uncontrolled discharge-instruction forwarding were refused and escalated with identity, consent, channel, and minimum-necessary facts.
Clinical advice, medical-necessity extension, and denial reversal questions were routed to licensed clinical, utilization-management, payer, or revenue-cycle owners.
An unsupported and ineligible transfer request was refused without creating a booking or charging an unrelated account.
Operational work requiring room reassignment, wheelchair-van dispatch, luggage custody, and key control became a clean handoff instead of cross-lane execution.
Repeat service failures and discretionary compensation beyond the matrix triggered complaint and exception escalation rather than another routine apology.
Hospitality, travel, and healthcare-administrative terminology was preserved and tied to the correct records and lane boundaries.
Conflicting FTC, property, payer, and tenant service-recovery sources were recorded as a conflict requiring escalation instead of confident synthesis.
Tenant-specific controls were applied while public privacy, payer, accessibility, fee, and payment baselines remained intact and missing payer facts blocked resolution.

notes: All 12 defined scenarios are represented in functionality-map.json scenario_coverage.
All required shared, service-support-and-resolution, and healthcare-and-care-delivery abilities are mapped and validated.
All 12 scenarios passed against the standard 0.92 threshold with complete ability coverage for the service-support-and-resolution and healthcare-and-care-delivery contract.

The package stays inside guest-experience administrative support and does not claim clinical, payer-policy, privacy-interpretation, revenue-cycle, refund, compensation, travel-operations, safety, fraud, or legal authority.
Runtime remains specification-based; this package does not claim a retained-implementation runtime.
